ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03315763 (ice cream soda) is the closest available class; no exact FoodOn entry exists for Coke Float specifically. Match is analogous.
ontology.foodex2_description: Carbonated soft drink with ice cream (ice cream float / spider)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated Coke Float code. A0CKP (carbonated soft drink) used as the closest defensible anchor; ice cream component noted in description.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for Coke Float or ice cream soda beverage in the clinical terminology hierarchy.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard glass of Coke Float (approximately 350 g) consists of roughly 280–300 ml of Coca-Cola topped with one scoop of vanilla ice cream, served chilled.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy — Carbonated beverage, cola (FDC ID 174852) and Ice creams, vanilla (FDC ID 167574), composite estimate
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for carbonated cola beverage (FDC ID 174852) and vanilla ice cream (FDC ID 167574), blended in an approximate 80:20 ratio (cola:ice cream by weight) consistent with a standard 350 g Coke Float serving. The input per_100g values from the GrabFood listing are broadly consistent with this USDA composite and have been validated against it; USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy is the authoritative nutrient reference. GrabFood is not used as a primary nutrient source per Project Veritas source policy.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; University of Sydney GI Database entries for carbonated cola beverage and vanilla ice cream
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Coke Float as a composite beverage. GI estimated from two University of Sydney GI Database approved-source components: (1) Coca-Cola GI = 63 (University of Sydney GI Database; carbonated cola beverage, glucose reference); (2) vanilla ice cream GI = 57 (University of Sydney GI Database). Weighted composite GI = 63 (cola-dominant 80:20 blend by weight). Glycemic load per 350 g serving = (63 × 42.0) / 100 = 26.5 g, where available carbohydrate per serving = 12.0 g/100g × 350 g / 100 = 42.0 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (11.43 g/100 g) from HFCS in Coca-Cola and added ice cream sugar; caffeine-mediated acute blood pressure elevation
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should avoid Coke Float entirely: the 11.43 g sugar/100 g (39.0 g per 350 g serving) far exceeds the AHA 2018 SSB threshold of 5 g/100 g, and the caffeine in Coca-Cola causes acute blood pressure elevation independent of sodium content.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very high sugar load (11.43 g/100 g; 39.0 g per serving) from liquid HFCS and ice cream sugar; high glycemic load of 26.5 per serving; zero dietary fibre
type2_diabetes.guidance: Patients with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid Coke Float: the glycemic load of 26.5 per standard 350 g serving, combined with zero dietary fibre and rapid liquid-sugar absorption from HFCS, causes a pronounced postprandial glucose spike inconsistent with ADA blood glucose management targets.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High added sugar (11.43 g/100 g), saturated fat (1.43 g/100 g), trans fat (0.09 g/100 g), and ultra-processed formulation
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should avoid Coke Float: the combination of high added sugar from HFCS, saturated fat and trans fat from ice cream, and the ultra-processed nature of both components is associated with elevated triglycerides, LDL cholesterol, and cardiovascular risk per AHA dietary guidelines.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Pro-inflammatory sugar load (11.43 g/100 g), saturated fat from ice cream, zero anti-inflammatory nutrients
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid Coke Float: the high refined sugar content promotes systemic inflammation, saturated fat from ice cream adds to the pro-inflammatory profile, and the beverage provides no anti-inflammatory nutrients (no omega-3, no polyphenols, no fibre).

llm_training.natural_language_description: A Coke Float (also known as a Coca-Cola Float or ice cream soda) is a chilled beverage assembled by pouring Coca-Cola over one or more scoops of vanilla ice cream, served in a tall glass. The carbonation of the cola causes the ice cream to foam and partially melt, creating a layered drink with a creamy, fizzy texture and a sweet, vanilla-cola flavour profile. No cooking is required; preparation is purely assembly-based. In Singapore, Coke Floats are a popular fast-food and casual-dining beverage, commonly offered at food courts, fast-food chains, and café-style restaurants such as the 959D outlet referenced in this record. The dish is classified as ultra-processed due to the HFCS-sweetened carbonated cola and commercially produced ice cream. Nutritionally, a 350 g serving delivers approximately 250 kcal, 42 g of sugar, 8 g of fat, and 3.5 g of protein, with a glycemic load of approximately 26.5 — placing it in the high-concern category for Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular disease management. Dietary fibre is absent. The beverage contains dairy (milk) allergens from the ice cream component and is not suitable for vegans or the lactose-intolerant. It is considered vegetarian and is generally halal-compliant when standard commercial ice cream is used.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a Coke Float?
Is Coke Float suitable for diabetics?
Can I drink Coke Float if I have high blood pressure?
What is the sugar content of a Coke Float?
What allergens are in a Coke Float?
What is the glycemic index of a Coke Float?
How much sugar is in a 350ml Coke Float?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are consistent with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy composite estimates for carbonated cola beverage (FDC ID 174852) and vanilla ice cream (FDC ID 167574) in an 80:20 blend ratio. The input GrabFood values (confidence 0.7) align with USDA reference data and have been validated against it. Confidence is medium rather than high because no exact single-source USDA entry exists for Coke Float as a composite dish; values are adapted from two component entries.
notes: 1. Nutrient values adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy: carbonated cola beverage (FDC ID 174852) and vanilla ice cream (FDC ID 167574) in an approximate 80:20 weight ratio. Input GrabFood per_100g values are consistent with this composite and retained as the working values. GrabFood is not used as a primary nutrient source per Project Veritas source policy. 2. The following per_100g fields are set to -1 (data not available): monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g. Reason: detailed fatty acid fractions, full micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this composite beverage preparation (80:20 cola-to-ice-cream blend) are not individually reported as a combined product in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy as of 2024. The blended format does not correspond to a single FDC entry, and disaggregated component-level data for these micronutrients was not available in sufficient detail to produce a defensible composite figure. 3. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero: Coca-Cola contains no dietary fibre by formulation, and vanilla ice cream contains negligible fibre — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for both components. vitamin_c_mg is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for both cola and vanilla ice cream components. 4. Cultural metadata in the input JSON incorrectly assigned Japanese cuisine and Japanese/Sushi/Tempura ethnic tags to this beverage; corrected to Singaporean/International. Dietary tags 'vegetarian' and 'gluten_free' from input reviewed and retained as accurate. 5. Two duplicate default portions (both 350 g) were present in the input; consolidated to a single 350 g standard glass serving. 6. GI of 63 is an analogous estimate weighted from University of Sydney GI Database component values for Coca-Cola (GI = 63) and vanilla ice cream (GI = 57) in an 80:20 blend. Glycemic load per 350 g serving = 26.5 g.

q1_food_identity.notes: food_name set to canonical English 'Coke Float'. Singaporean cuisine multilingual names present: Chinese simplified and traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi. Input menu name '959D Coke Float(D)' retained as alias.

q2_core_macro_completeness_and_source_in.notes: energy_kcal (71.43), protein_g (1.0), fat_g (2.29), carbohydrate_g (12.0) all present and numeric. nutrient_source references USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy FDC IDs 174852 and 167574 explicitly. GrabFood is documented as not used as primary source.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g (0.0 confirmed zero), sodium_mg (17.14), saturated_fat_g (1.43), sugar_g (11.43) all present as numeric values consistent with USDA SR Legacy reference data.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index (63), gi_category ('medium'), glycemic_load_per_serving (26.5), gi_source fully populated referencing University of Sydney GI Database. Analogous estimate basis documented with component GI values and calculation shown.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodOn FOODON_03315763 (ice cream soda) used as closest_parent match with gap note. FoodEx2 A0CKP (carbonated soft drink) used as closest_parent anchor with gap note. SNOMED CT null with gap note. No codes invented.

q6_llm_ready_semantics.notes: natural_language_description is over 1,100 characters, well above the 200-character minimum. Covers food identity, preparation method, taste and texture, cultural context, clinical relevance, and key nutrients.

q7_health_context.notes: All three required conditions present: Hypertension (high concern), Type 2 Diabetes (human_condition_name correct, machine key type2_diabetes), Cardiovascular Disease (high concern). Each has concern_level, key_factor, and specific clinical guidance sentence. Rheumatoid Arthritis retained as additional condition.

q8_aliases_and_multilingual_synonym_cove.notes: aliases flat array contains 13 entries covering English variants, Chinese simplified and traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi, and romanized forms. aliases_by_language populated for all relevant languages. All aliases_by_language string values verified present verbatim in flat aliases array.
